steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the structure of the passage: it opens with a specific historical example (Jennie Carter's 1867 journalism), then describes an organization that preserves and honors that history (the NABJ).

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — It summarizes a theory about journalism, then explains how a journalistic practice has changed over time.: the passage does the opposite — it opens with Carter's 1867 journalism as the specific example first, then broadens to the NABJ as the preserving organization. The order is reversed. ✗
  • Choice B — It mentions a specific achievement in Black journalistic history, then describes an organization dedicated to upholding that history.: matches exactly — Carter's 1867 work is the specific achievement; the NABJ, founded to honor journalists like Carter, is the organization. ✓
  • Choice C — It introduces the history of an organization honoring Black journalists, then suggests how that organization might recruit more members.: the passage presents no problem to be solved; it celebrates Carter's achievement and the organization that honors it. ✗
  • Choice D — It describes the career of a well-known figure in Black journalism, then compares that career to one of a figure who is lesser known.: the passage presents one coherent perspective, not competing viewpoints. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
